title: Lemma C (necessity): existence forces R=(I+G)^{-1} to be completely-S (Reiman-Williams boundary property)

comments.author: human
comments.body:
**Definitions.** $R$ is an $\mathcal S$-matrix if $\exists x\ge0$ with $Rx>0$. $R$ is completely-$\mathcal S$ if every principal submatrix $R_J$ ($J\subseteq\{1,\dots,d\}$) is $\mathcal S$.

**Claim (necessity):** If a process $(W^\ast,Y^\ast)$ satisfying (i)-(iv) exists, then $R=(I+G)^{-1}$ must be completely-$\mathcal S$.

**Difficulty:** This is the Reiman-Williams boundary property and is the deepest analytic ingredient. One must analyze the behaviour of $W^\ast$ near intersections of faces $\bigcap_{j\in J}\{w_j=0\}$ and derive a contradiction (the process cannot be pushed back into the orthant / cannot remain a continuous semimartingale in $\mathbb R^d_+$) whenever some principal submatrix $R_J$ fails to be $\mathcal S$.

Reference: Reiman & Williams, PTRF 77 (1988) 87-97 (correction PTRF 80 (1989) 633).
